question: He said, 'I am hungry.'
options: He said that he was hungry.
He said that I am hungry.
He said that he is hungry.
He told that he was hungry.
answer: He said that he was hungry.
explanation: In indirect speech, the present simple 'am' changes to the past simple 'was' when the reporting verb is in the past. ✅

question: She said, 'I work in a bank.'
options: She said that she works in a bank.
She said that she worked in a bank.
She said that I worked in a bank.
She told she worked in a bank.
answer: She said that she worked in a bank.
explanation: Simple Present (work) changes to Simple Past (worked). ✅

question: They said, 'We are playing cricket.'
options: They said that they were playing cricket.
They said that we were playing cricket.
They said that they are playing cricket.
They told that they played cricket.
answer: They said that they were playing cricket.
explanation: Present Continuous (are playing) changes to Past Continuous (were playing). ✅

question: The teacher said, 'The sun rises in the east.'
options: The teacher said that the sun rose in the east.
The teacher said that the sun rises in the east.
The teacher told that the sun rises in the east.
The teacher says that the sun rises in the east.
answer: The teacher said that the sun rises in the east.
explanation: Universal truths or habitual facts do not change their tense in indirect speech. ✅

question: He said to me, 'You are late.'
options: He said to me that I was late.
He told me that I was late.
He told me that you were late.
He said that I am late.
answer: He told me that I was late.
explanation: 'Said to' changes to 'told' when followed by an object. 'You' changes to 'I' according to the object 'me'. ✅

question: Ram said, 'I have completed my work.'
options: Ram said that he has completed his work.
Ram said that he had completed his work.
Ram said that I had completed my work.
Ram told that he had completed his work.
answer: Ram said that he had completed his work.
explanation: Present Perfect (have completed) changes to Past Perfect (had completed). ✅

question: She said, 'I can speak English.'
options: She said that she could speak English.
She said that she can speak English.
She told that she can speak English.
She said that I could speak English.
answer: She said that she could speak English.
explanation: 'Can' changes to 'could' in indirect speech. ✅

question: He said, 'I will go to Delhi tomorrow.'
options: He said that he will go to Delhi the next day.
He said that he would go to Delhi tomorrow.
He said that he would go to Delhi the next day.
He told that he would go to Delhi the next day.
answer: He said that he would go to Delhi the next day.
explanation: 'Will' changes to 'would' and 'tomorrow' changes to 'the next day'. ✅

question: My father said, 'Honesty is the best policy.'
options: My father said that honesty was the best policy.
My father said that honesty is the best policy.
My father told that honesty is the best policy.
My father said that honesty has been the best policy.
answer: My father said that honesty is the best policy.
explanation: Proverbs and general truths remain in the present tense. ✅

question: They said, 'We have been living here for ten years.'
options: They said that they had been living there for ten years.
They said that they have been living there for ten years.
They said that they were living there for ten years.
They told that they had been living here for ten years.
answer: They said that they had been living there for ten years.
explanation: Present Perfect Continuous changes to Past Perfect Continuous, and 'here' changes to 'there'. ✅

question: She said to him, 'I am your friend.'
options: She told him that she was his friend.
She said to him that she was my friend.
She told him that I was his friend.
She told him that she is his friend.
answer: She told him that she was his friend.
explanation: 'Am' becomes 'was', and pronouns change to match the speaker and listener. ✅

question: He said, 'I went to the market yesterday.'
options: He said that he had gone to the market yesterday.
He said that he went to the market the previous day.
He said that he had gone to the market the previous day.
He told that he went to the market the day before.
answer: He said that he had gone to the market the previous day.
explanation: Simple Past (went) changes to Past Perfect (had gone), and 'yesterday' becomes 'the previous day'. ✅

question: The boy said, 'I am doing my homework.'
options: The boy said that he is doing his homework.
The boy said that he was doing his homework.
The boy told that he was doing my homework.
The boy said that he had been doing his homework.
answer: The boy said that he was doing his homework.
explanation: Present Continuous becomes Past Continuous. ✅

question: Ravi said, 'I may buy a car.'
options: Ravi said that he may buy a car.
Ravi said that he might buy a car.
Ravi told that he might buy a car.
Ravi said that I might buy a car.
answer: Ravi said that he might buy a car.
explanation: 'May' changes to 'might'. ✅

question: She said, 'The earth is round.'
options: She said that the earth was round.
She said that the earth is round.
She told that the earth is round.
She said the earth being round.
answer: She said that the earth is round.
explanation: Geographical facts remain unchanged in tense. ✅

question: They said, 'We shall win the match.'
options: They said that they shall win the match.
They said that they should win the match.
They said that they would win the match.
They told that they will win the match.
answer: They said that they would win the match.
explanation: 'Shall' in direct speech generally changes to 'would' in indirect speech when it denotes future intent for 'They'. ✅

question: He said, 'I don't like coffee.'
options: He said that he doesn't like coffee.
He said that he did not like coffee.
He told that he did not like coffee.
He said that I did not like coffee.
answer: He said that he did not like coffee.
explanation: Do not/Does not changes to Did not. ✅

question: Anil said to me, 'I am busy now.'
options: Anil told me that he was busy now.
Anil told me that he was busy then.
Anil said to me that he was busy then.
Anil told me that I was busy then.
answer: Anil told me that he was busy then.
explanation: 'Now' changes to 'then'. ✅

question: She said, 'I was writing a letter.'
options: She said that she had been writing a letter.
She said that she was writing a letter.
She told that she has been writing a letter.
She said that she wrote a letter.
answer: She said that she had been writing a letter.
explanation: Past Continuous (was writing) changes to Past Perfect Continuous (had been writing). ✅

question: The servant said, 'I have cleaned the room.'
options: The servant said that he cleaned the room.
The servant said that he has cleaned the room.
The servant said that he had cleaned the room.
The servant told that he had cleaned the room.
answer: The servant said that he had cleaned the room.
explanation: Present Perfect ➔ Past Perfect. ✅

question: He said, 'Honest people are always happy.'
options: He said that honest people were always happy.
He said that honest people are always happy.
He told that honest people are always happy.
He says that honest people are always happy.
answer: He said that honest people are always happy.
explanation: General statements about character often remain in present tense. ✅

question: She said, 'I will help you.'
options: She said that she will help me.
She said that she would help me.
She said that I would help you.
She told me she would help me.
answer: She said that she would help me.
explanation: Will ➔ Would. ✅

question: The boy said, 'I like to play chess.'
options: The boy said that he likes to play chess.
The boy said that he liked to play chess.
The boy told that he liked to play chess.
The boy said that I liked to play chess.
answer: The boy said that he liked to play chess.
explanation: Simple Present ➔ Simple Past. ✅

question: They said, 'We are going on a picnic.'
options: They said that they were going on a picnic.
They said that we were going on a picnic.
They said that they are going on a picnic.
They told that they had gone on a picnic.
answer: They said that they were going on a picnic.
explanation: Are going ➔ Were going. ✅

question: He said to his sister, 'I am waiting for you.'
options: He told his sister that he was waiting for her.
He told his sister that I was waiting for you.
He said to his sister that he is waiting for her.
He told his sister that he had been waiting for her.
answer: He told his sister that he was waiting for her.
explanation: Present Continuous ➔ Past Continuous + pronoun shift. ✅

question: The teacher said, 'Two and two make four.'
options: The teacher said that two and two made four.
The teacher said that two and two make four.
The teacher told that two and two make four.
The teacher said two and two making four.
answer: The teacher said that two and two make four.
explanation: Mathematical facts do not change tense. ✅

question: She said, 'I have a headache.'
options: She said that she had a headache.
She said that she has a headache.
She told that she had a headache.
She said that I have a headache.
answer: She said that she had a headache.
explanation: Have (Present) ➔ Had (Past). ✅

question: The gardener said, 'The flowers are blooming.'
options: The gardener said that the flowers were blooming.
The gardener said that the flowers are blooming.
The gardener told that the flowers bloomed.
The gardener said flowers blooming.
answer: The gardener said that the flowers were blooming.
explanation: Present Continuous ➔ Past Continuous. ✅

question: He said, 'I can do this work.'
options: He said that he can do that work.
He said that he could do this work.
He said that he could do that work.
He told that he could do that work.
answer: He said that he could do that work.
explanation: Can ➔ Could, and 'this' ➔ 'that'. ✅

question: She said, 'I have seen this movie before.'
options: She said that she has seen this movie before.
She said that she had seen that movie before.
She said that she saw that movie before.
She told that she had seen this movie before.
answer: She said that she had seen that movie before.
explanation: Present Perfect ➔ Past Perfect, and 'this' ➔ 'that'. ✅
